deferredReason: Delivered 2026-06-08 in one cross-cutting pass (Go primary surface + Python parity mirror together), preserving spec 081's byte-for-byte parity invariant. gherkinHash, linkedTests, and evidenceRefs are populated below; RED->GREEN + parity evidence is in report.md (DELIVERY PASS / VALIDATE RE-VERIFICATION / CLOSE-OUT AUTHORING PASS).

scenarios.title: Go dead-letter Smackerel-Last-Error: CRLF-laden error yields exactly six canonical headers, zero injected

gherkin.given: a Go subscriber exhausts a message with lastError "boom\r\nNats-Msg-Id: forged"
gherkin.then: the message has exactly the six canonical Smackerel-* header lines and no injected header line

scenarios.title: Removing the Go sanitizer reproduces the header injection (adversarial regression)

gherkin.given: the CR/LF sanitizer is absent from the Go subscribers
gherkin.when: a "boom\r\nNats-Msg-Id: forged" lastError is built into headers
gherkin.then: a seventh injected header line appears and the regression test fails RED

scenarios.title: Python dead-letter Smackerel-Last-Error: CRLF-laden str(exc) yields exactly six canonical headers, zero injected

gherkin.given: the ML poison handler exhausts a message with str(exc) "boom\r\nNats-Msg-Id: forged"
gherkin.then: the message has exactly the six canonical Smackerel-* header lines and no injected header line

scenarios.title: Go and Python sanitized Smackerel-Last-Error values are byte-for-byte equal (parity, adversarial)

gherkin.given: the same CR/LF-and-control-laden error string on both runtimes
gherkin.when: each builds its Smackerel-Last-Error header value
gherkin.then: the two sanitized values are byte-for-byte equal and a one-sided fix fails this assertion
